zh.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers是William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby和Philippa Clifford的女兒，出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾格洛比。她於1407年10月28日與John Greystoke結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1460年在英國肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆，埋葬在英國肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers是William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby和Philippa Clifford的女儿，出生在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾格洛比。她于1407年10月28日与John Greystoke结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1460年在英国肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆，埋葬在英国肯特韋斯特 比可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers was born in Groby, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby and Philippa Clifford. She married John Greystoke on 28 October 1407. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1460, in West Peckham, Kent, England, and was buried in West Peckham, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers est née à Groby, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby et Philippa Clifford. Elle a épousé John Greystoke le 28 octobre 1407. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1460, à West Peckham, Kent, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à West Peckham, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers wurde in Groby, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby und Philippa Clifford geboren. Sie hat am 28. Oktober 1407 John Greystoke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1460 in West Peckham, Kent, England und wurde in West Peckham, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers è nata il nel Groby, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby e Philippa Clifford. Lei sposato John Greystoke il 28 ottobre 1407. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1460, nel West Peckham, Kent, England, e fu sepolta a West Peckham, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de FerrersはイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアグロビーでWilliam Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of GrobyとPhilippa Cliffordの娘として生まれた。1407年10月28日にJohn Greystokeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1460年にイギリスケントウエスト ペックハムで死去し，イギリスケントウエスト ペックハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 그로비에서 William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby(와)과 Philippa Clifford의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1407년 10월 28일 John Greystoke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 영국 켄트 웨스트 펙함에서 사망했으며 영국 켄트 웨스트 펙함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers nasceu em Groby, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filha de William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby e Philippa Clifford. Ela casou-se com John Greystoke em 28 de outubro de 1407. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1460, em West Peckham, Kent, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em West Peckham, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers родилась в местности гроби, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby и Philippa Clifford. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Greystoke 28 октября 1407 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1460 г. в местности вест пеккхам, кэнт, Англия и была похоронена в местности вест пеккхам, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth de Ferrers nació en Groby, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hija de William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby y Philippa Clifford. Se casó con John Greystoke el 28 de octubre de 1407. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1460, en West Peckham, Kent, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en West Peckham, Kent, Inglaterra.
